_comment: Tier-1 objective gates derived from JTBD-1 (the deterministic, MLS-safe cinematic listing slideshow) + the ffmpeg-slideshow discipline. Video expectations are graded by ffprobe (duration / dimensions / streams) and by VIEWING sampled frames of the actual MP4 (vision) — never from filenames or text. The MLS-safety assertion requires confirming the listing photos appear VERBATIM (only the virtual camera moved; no melted/invented geometry, no pixel synthesis) and that the first-frame AB-723 disclosure card is burned into ~the first 3s. Fixtures: evals/fixtures/photo1.png + photo2.png are two real room photos.

evals.prompt: For the listing maple-st, make a cinematic listing video from the two photos in inputs/ (photo1.png, photo2.png). listing.json: {"address":"123 Maple St","price":"$899,000","beds":3,"baths":2,"sqft":1850,"agent":"Jamie Rivera","cta":"Schedule a tour today"}.
evals.expected_output: Both exports/listing-16x9.mp4 (1920x1080) and exports/listing-9x16.mp4 (1080x1920) are produced by the keyless ffmpeg slideshow: a branded intro card (address '123 Maple St' as the accent title, price·beds·baths·sqft, agent), one slow single-axis Ken-Burns segment over EACH real photo (the photo shipped verbatim — only the camera moves), and a CTA outro ('Schedule a tour today'). The first-frame AB-723 disclosure card is burned into ~the first 3s of each export. disclosure.md is written from disclosure-stamp with the MLS remark + AB-723 line + the reachable-original note (the real photos ARE the unaltered originals). No fal/ai-gen call was made.
expectations: artifacts/maple-st/exports/listing-16x9.mp4 exists, ffprobes as 1920x1080 H.264 with an audio stream, and (sampled frames viewed) is a slideshow of the TWO real input photos with a slow single-axis camera move over each — the photos appear VERBATIM (no melted, warped, or invented geometry; no pixel synthesis), plus an intro card showing '123 Maple St' and an outro CTA card
artifacts/maple-st/exports/listing-9x16.mp4 exists and ffprobes as 1080x1920 (the vertical reel cut of the same slideshow), intro hook within the first ~0-2s, total duration roughly 15-40s

the first ~3s of EACH export carries the burned first-frame AB-723 disclosure card (the 'Video created from listing photos using AI motion technology' text, viewed) — produced by assemble-listing.sh, not skipped

artifacts/maple-st/disclosure.md exists and was produced via the disclosure-stamp skill (stamp.py --type virtual-staging): it contains the verbatim first-frame CARD_TEXT, the ready-to-paste MLS remark, the AB-723 disclosure line, and the reachable-original note stating the real listing photos are the unaltered originals (camera-motion only, no separately-altered original to host)
the run was KEYLESS — no ai-gen / fal generative call was made (pure ffmpeg); the assembler's JSON verdict (PASS, or FLAG with reasons) is recorded in state.md

evals.prompt: For the listing oak-ave, build a vertical listing reel from inputs/photo1.png and inputs/photo2.png — no music, and the listing.json has no sqft and no cta. listing.json: {"address":"88 Oak Ave","price":"$1,250,000","beds":4,"baths":3,"agent":"Priya Shah"}.
evals.expected_output: A 9:16 reel (1080x1920) is produced as a SILENT export (no --music — no track was auto-fetched). The intro card shows '88 Oak Ave' + '$1,250,000 · 4 bd · 3 ba' (the missing sqft line is OMITTED, never rendered as 'undefined'). Because cta is missing, the outro CTA defaults to the agent name 'Priya Shah'. The first-frame AB-723 card is burned in and disclosure.md is written. The photos are shipped verbatim.
expectations: artifacts/oak-ave/exports/listing-9x16.mp4 exists, ffprobes as 1080x1920, and is a SILENT slideshow (the audio stream is silence — no licensed/auto-fetched music was added because none was supplied)
the intro card (viewed) shows '88 Oak Ave' as the accent title and the facts line shows price/beds/baths but OMITS sqft entirely — the literal string 'undefined' never appears anywhere on screen
an outro card is present and its CTA falls back to the agent name 'Priya Shah' because listing.json had no cta field (the omission/default is recorded as an assumption)
the first ~3s carries the burned first-frame AB-723 disclosure card, and the two real photos appear verbatim with only a slow single-axis camera move (no synthesized geometry)
artifacts/oak-ave/disclosure.md is written via disclosure-stamp with the MLS remark + AB-723 line + reachable-original note

evals.prompt: Make a listing video for elm-ct from these photos, but inputs/ has the photos and NO listing.json at all.
evals.expected_output: A clean recorded FAILURE — not a question and not an invented listing. Because listing.json is the required facts source for the intro/outro cards, the skill records the missing-required-input in state.md (status: blocked, next_action: re-run onboarding — inputs/listing.json missing or unparseable) and stops. It does NOT fabricate an address/price, does NOT generate any video, and does NOT prompt the user (headless).
expectations: no exports/*.mp4 is produced for elm-ct — the run stops at the required-input gate rather than guessing card text
state.md (or the run log) records a clean blocked failure for the slideshow phase with next_action pointing back to onboarding because inputs/listing.json is missing/unparseable

the skill did NOT invent an address, price, or agent, did NOT generate a video from text, and did NOT ask the user a question (it runs headless — a missing required input is a recorded failure, not a prompt)
